title: Per-Job GPU Profile (DCGM + OTel)
description: R20 (v5 review) — per-job × per-GPU × full-lifecycle profile. Drill-down view scoped by $hostname (the worker node Slurm allocated to the job) and $job_id (used to deep-link into Tempo traces). DCGM metrics are per-host/per-GPU (the dcgm-exporter does not carry slurm_job_id labels by default); operator provisioning-latency exemplars link to the OTel trace so the user can pivot to the full job lifecycle span tree.

list.name: hostname
list.label: Worker node
list.description: DCGM Hostname label. Pick the Slurm worker pod that ran your job (e.g. slurm-worker-gpu-rtx4070-0). Find it via `squeue -j <jobid> -o '%N'`.

list.name: gpu
list.label: GPU index
list.description: Per-host GPU ordinal (0..N-1). Set to .* to show all GPUs on the host.

list.name: job_id
list.label: Slurm job_id
list.description: Used to deep-link into Tempo. Set freely; this dashboard does not query Slurm by job_id (slurm-exporter exposes aggregate metrics only — drill into Tempo for per-job trace).

options.content:
1. **Find your job's worker node**: `squeue -j $job_id -o '%N'` (or check `sacct -j $job_id`)
2. Pick that hostname in the **Worker node** dropdown above; leave **GPU index** as `All` unless you only want one device.
3. Type the Slurm `job_id` in the textbox to populate the **Open Tempo trace** link panel below.
4. The GPU panels show DCGM SM%/VRAM/copy-util/power/temp for the selected host across the time window — overlay your job's [start, end] times to read its profile.
5. The provisioning-latency panel shows operator scale-up events; click any **exemplar dot** to jump into the OTel trace for that decision in Tempo.

panels.title: SM Utilisation (%)
panels.description: DCGM_FI_DEV_GPU_UTIL — true streaming-multiprocessor busy percentage. Sustained < 40% suggests compute-bound bottlenecks elsewhere (data pipeline, all-reduce, ckpt I/O).

panels.title: VRAM Used (MiB)
panels.description: DCGM_FI_DEV_FB_USED — frame-buffer in use. Compare against device total for over/under-provisioning analysis.

panels.title: Memory Copy Util (%)
panels.description: DCGM_FI_DEV_MEM_COPY_UTIL — memory-controller busy ratio. High values during DDP all-reduce phases indicate bandwidth-bound training.

panels.title: Power Draw (W)
panels.description: DCGM_FI_DEV_POWER_USAGE — current board power. Useful for spotting throttling (sustained at TDP limit) or idle waste.

panels.title: Temperature (°C)
panels.description: DCGM_FI_DEV_GPU_TEMP — die temperature. Sustained > 85 °C on consumer cards (RTX 4070) suggests airflow problems.

panels.title: Pool provisioning latency (p95) — click exemplar dot to open Tempo trace
panels.description: slurm_operator_provisioning_latency_seconds_bucket — wall-clock seconds from scale_up_decision to pod readyReplicas reaching target, per pool. Exemplars carry the OTel traceID and link directly into Tempo so you can see the full submit → queue → running → provisioning span tree.

options.content:
**[Search Tempo for traces tagged `job_id=$job_id`](/explore?left=%5B%22now-3h%22,%22now%22,%22tempo%22,%7B%22queryType%22:%22traceqlSearch%22,%22filters%22:%5B%7B%22id%22:%221%22,%22tag%22:%22job_id%22,%22operator%22:%22%3D%22,%22value%22:%5B%22$job_id%22%5D,%22valueType%22:%22string%22%7D%5D%7D%5D)** — opens Grafana Explore with Tempo TraceQL pre-filled. Requires Phase 7-A (monitoring.otel.enabled=true) and a job_id span attribute on the root span emitted by serve.py's /decide handler.

For the operator's view of this job (scale_up_decision + k8s_provisioning spans), exemplars on the p95 latency panel above will deep-link into the same trace.
